section_title: If an agent, without the knowledge of his principal, deals in the business
content:
216. If an agent, without the knowledge of his principal, deals in the business
right to
of the agency on his own account instead of on account of his principal, the
benefit
gained by principal is entitled to claim from the agent any benefit which may have
agent
resulted to him from the transaction.

his own Illustration
account in
business of A directs B, his agent, to buy a certain house for him. B tells A it cannot be
agency
bought, and buys the house for himself. A may, on discovering that B has
bought the house, compel him to sell it to A at the price he gave for it.
